title: Understanding the Problem
text: The F-35B Lightning II takes off vertically to a height of $$20.00$$ $$m$$ and then follows a path angled at $$30$$ degrees for $$20.00$$ km. We need to find the final displacement in terms of horizontal and vertical components.

title: Breaking Down the Path
text: The path after the vertical takeoff is at a 30-degree angle. We can use trigonometry to find the horizontal and vertical components of this path.

title: Calculating Horizontal Component
text: Use the cosine function to find the horizontal component of the $$20.00$$ km path. The formula is: horizontal component $$=$$ 20.00*cos(30 degrees).

title: Calculating Vertical Component
text: Use the sine function to find the vertical component of the $$20.00$$ km path. The formula is: vertical component $$=$$ 20.00*sin(30 degrees).

title: Adding the Initial Vertical Displacement
text: Don't forget to add the initial vertical displacement of $$20.00$$ $$m$$ (or $$0.02$$ km) to the vertical component calculated from the angled path.

title: Final Displacement Vector
text: Combine the horizontal and vertical components to form the final displacement vector.

title: Final Displacement Calculation
text: The final displacement vector is given by combining the horizontal and vertical components: $$17.32i+10.02j$$ km.
